title: Week 05 - Factors, Multiples, and Prime Numbers
description: Practice multiple checks, factor enumeration, prime checks, greatest common factors, and least common multiples.

questions.title: Check Whether One Number Is a Multiple of Another

description.background: In a school activity, some numbers need to be grouped by a fixed interval. If a number can be divided evenly by base, it is a multiple of base.
description.task: Determine whether number is a multiple of base.
description.output: Return true if number is a multiple of base; otherwise return false.
description.hint: You can observe the remainder after division.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(number, base)
    if number % base == 0 then
        return true
    end
    return false
end

questions.title: Check Whether a Team Can Be Split into Equal Groups

description.background: In PE class there are people students, and the teacher wants to split them evenly into groups of groupSize students each.
description.task: Determine whether these students can be split into equal groups with no one left over.
description.output: Return true if they can be split evenly; otherwise return false.
description.hint: Equal grouping means no student can be left over.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(people, groupSize)
    if people % groupSize == 0 then
        return true
    end
    return false
end

questions.title: Count Multiples of a Target Number from 1 to n

description.background: The teacher gives a target number k and asks students to find all multiples of k from 1 to n.
description.task: Count how many numbers from 1 to n are multiples of k.
description.output: Return the count of multiples of k.
description.hint: Check each number one by one against the multiple rule.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n, k)
    local count = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        if i % k == 0 then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end

    return count
end

questions.title: Find How Many Equal-Sharing Methods a Puzzle Block Count Has

description.background: A puzzle box has blocks pieces. The teacher wants to know how many different group counts can divide these puzzle pieces evenly.
description.task: Count the number of factors of blocks.
description.output: Return the number of possible group counts that can divide the blocks evenly.
description.hint: You can try each possible group count one by one.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(blocks)
    local count = 0

    for i = 1, blocks do
        if blocks % i == 0 then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end

    return count
end

questions.title: Count How Many Factors a Special Number Has

description.background: In a game, a special number needs to be checked to see how many positive integers can divide it evenly.
description.task: Count the number of factors of number.
description.output: Return the number of factors of number.
description.hint: A factor must divide the original number evenly.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(number)
    local count = 0

    for i = 1, number do
        if number % i == 0 then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end

    return count
end

questions.title: Calculate the Count and Sum of All Factors of a Number

description.background: The teacher wants to know not only how many factors a number has, but also what those factors add up to.
description.task: Count the factors of number and calculate the sum of all factors.
description.output: Return the factor count and factor sum in order.
description.hint: After finding a factor, you can update both the count and the sum.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(number)
    local count = 0
    local sum = 0

    for i = 1, number do
        if number % i == 0 then
            count = count + 1
            sum = sum + i
        end
    end

    return count, sum
end

questions.title: Check Whether a Special Number Is Prime

description.background: In a game, a prime number is a number that has exactly two factors.
description.output: Return true if it is prime; otherwise return false.
description.hint: You can count the factors first, then decide the number type from that count.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(number)
    local factorCount = 0

    for i = 1, number do
        if number % i == 0 then
            factorCount = factorCount + 1
        end
    end

    if factorCount == 2 then
        return true
    end
    return false
end

questions.title: Find the Largest Common Group Size for Two Classes

description.background: Two classes have classA and classB students. The teacher wants both classes to be split evenly into groups and wants each group to be as large as possible.
description.task: Find the greatest common factor of classA and classB.
description.input: Two positive integers: classA and classB.
description.output: Return the largest common group size.
description.hint: Try all possible group sizes and keep the largest valid one found so far.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(classA, classB)
    local best = 1

    for i = 1, classA do
        if classA % i == 0 and classB % i == 0 then
            best = i
        end
    end

    return best
end

questions.title: Find the First Minute When Two Alarms Ring Together

description.background: Two alarms start timing at the same moment. The first rings every a minutes, and the second rings every b minutes. We need to find the first time when they ring together.
description.task: Find the least common multiple of a and b.
description.input: Two positive integers: a and b.
description.output: Return the first minute when the two alarms ring together.
description.hint: Start trying from smaller times and find the first time that matches both cycles.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(a, b)
    local answer = 0

    for i = 1, a * b do
        if answer == 0 and i % a == 0 and i % b == 0 then
            answer = i
        end
    end

    return answer
end
